subject: Mathematics
title: Countable Infinity
description: Learn Countable Infinity in one minute.

hook.text: How big is infinity? Cantor's first surprise: you can 'count' some infinite sets.

buildup.text: A set is countably infinite if you can list its members one by one, matching each to a natural number.

discovery.text: Even numbers are countable: pair 1 with 2, 2 with 4, 3 with 6. Every even number gets a partner.

twist.text: There are 'as many' even numbers as natural numbers. Removing half of infinity leaves the same infinity.

climax.text: Integers, rationals, and even algebraic numbers are all countably infinite—the same size.

punchline.text: Half of infinity is still the same infinity.

quiz.question: What does 'countably infinite' mean?
options: The elements can be matched one-to-one with natural numbers
They are smaller than any other infinity
